segments.label: Where you are
segments.script: In 1876, I founded the Gold Dollar, one of the first Black weekly newspapers in the South. I founded the Gold Dollar to educate and strengthen the Black community.

segments.label: Why it matters
segments.script: We were still living in the shadow of enslavement and this made reading, writing, and communication crucial keys to freedom.

segments.label: What is this place
segments.script: The Gold Dollar was the first Black-owned newspaper in this capital city, known by some as the first Black newspaper west of the Mississippi.

segments.label: Key fact
segments.script: Between 1870 and 1900, this was among 48 Black-owned commercial newspapers in Texas.

segments.label: Deeper history
segments.script: Nowadays, the Gold Dollar building is not just a former newspaper office, though.

segments.label: Wrap
segments.script: This is the last standing structure of Wheatville, a freedom colony in Central Austin.
